sections.content:
• Forward-looking statements are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially. (Evidence: We discuss many of these risks, uncertainties and other factors in this Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q and our Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended January 25, 2026 in greater detail un)
• NVIDIA's business is subject to risks associated with its expansion into data center-scale AI infrastructure. (Evidence: NVIDIA is now a data center-scale AI infrastructure company reshaping all industries.)

sections.title: Financial Overview
sections.content:
• Revenue: $81.6B (vs. $44.1B) — +85.2% YoY — Revenue nearly doubled, driven by data center-scale AI infrastructure demand.
• Gross Profit: $61.2B (vs. $26.7B) — +129.3% YoY — Gross profit more than doubled, with margin expansion of 14.4 percentage points.
• Operating Income: $53.5B (vs. $21.6B) — +147.4% YoY — Operating income surged, reflecting strong revenue growth and operating leverage.
• Net Income: $58.3B (vs. $18.8B) — +210.6% YoY — Net income more than tripled, benefiting from higher revenue and improved margins.
• Diluted EPS: $2.40 (vs. $0.77) — +211.7% YoY — EPS growth outpaced net income due to share repurchases.
• Gross Margin: 74.9% (vs. 60.5%) — +14.4pp — Significant margin expansion driven by product mix and scale.
• Operating Margin: 65.6% (vs. 49.1%) — +16.5pp — Operating margin improved sharply as revenue growth outpaced operating expenses.
• Net Margin: 71.5% (vs. 42.6%) — +28.9pp — Net margin benefited from higher operating income and other income.
• Operating Cash Flow: $50.3B (vs. $27.4B) — +83.7% YoY — Cash generation remained strong, funding investments and shareholder returns.
• Capital Expenditures: $1.8B (vs. $1.2B) — +43.2% YoY — CapEx increased to support infrastructure and product development.

sections.title: Management Commentary
sections.content:
Themes: - NVIDIA has evolved from PC graphics to a data center-scale AI infrastructure company.
- Sustained demand for 3D graphics and the scale of the gaming market provided a foundation for expansion into AI, data science, and autonomous vehicles.
- The company leverages its GPU architecture to create platforms for scientific computing, AI, and digital twin applications.
Quotes: - Speaker: Management (MD&A)
Quote: NVIDIA pioneered accelerated computing to help solve the most challenging computational problems.
Context: Describing the company's origin and expansion into computationally intensive fields.
- Speaker: Management (MD&A)
Quote: NVIDIA is now a data center-scale AI infrastructure company reshaping all industries.
Context: Highlighting the company's current strategic positioning.
Capital Allocation: - Capital expenditures increased to $1.8B from $1.2B, supporting infrastructure and product development.
- Free cash flow of $48.6B provides capacity for shareholder returns and strategic investments, though specific allocation details are not disclosed in the excerpts.
Source Section Ref: Item 2 – MD&A

summary.business_overview:
*Auto-generated from structured data because the writer output failed validation (Editorial summary length must be 80-280 words (current: 298).).*
## Executive Summary
NVIDIA reports Q1 FY2027 revenue of $81.6B, up 85.2% YoY, driven by data center-scale AI infrastructure demand. Management's disclosed tone was positive. Revenue surged to $81.6B from $44.1B in the prior-year quarter, reflecting continued momentum in AI and accelerated computing.; Net income more than tripled to $58.3B, with diluted EPS of $2.40 compared to $0.77 a year ago.; Gross margin expanded to 74.9% from 60.5%, underscoring operating leverage and product mix shift toward higher-value platforms.; Operating cash flow reached $50.3B, enabling robust free cash flow of $48.6B after $1.8B in capital expenditures.

## Financials
- Revenue: $81.6B vs. $44.1B (+85.2% YoY) – Revenue nearly doubled, driven by data center-scale AI infrastructure demand.
- Gross Profit: $61.2B vs. $26.7B (+129.3% YoY) – Gross profit more than doubled, with margin expansion of 14.4 percentage points.
- Operating Income: $53.5B vs. $21.6B (+147.4% YoY) – Operating income surged, reflecting strong revenue growth and operating leverage.
- Net Income: $58.3B vs. $18.8B (+210.6% YoY) – Net income more than tripled, benefiting from higher revenue and improved margins.
- Diluted EPS: $2.40 vs. $0.77 (+211.7% YoY) – EPS growth outpaced net income due to share repurchases.
- Gross Margin: 74.9% vs. 60.5% (+14.4pp) – Significant margin expansion driven by product mix and scale.
- Operating Margin: 65.6% vs. 49.1% (+16.5pp) – Operating margin improved sharply as revenue growth outpaced operating expenses.
- Net Margin: 71.5% vs. 42.6% (+28.9pp) – Net margin benefited from higher operating income and other income.
- Operating Cash Flow: $50.3B vs. $27.4B (+83.7% YoY) – Cash generation remained strong, funding investments and shareholder returns.
- Capital Expenditures: $1.8B vs. $1.2B (+43.2% YoY) – CapEx increased to support infrastructure and product development.
- Free Cash Flow: $48.6B vs. $26.2B (+85.5% YoY) – FCF nearly doubled, providing ample liquidity for strategic initiatives.
- Profitability: Gross margin expanded to 74.9% from 60.5%, driven by higher-margin data center products.; Operating margin reached 65.6%, up from 49.1%, as revenue growth significantly outpaced operating expense increases.; Net margin improved to 71.5% from 42.6%, reflecting strong operating performance and other income contributions.
- Cash flow: Operating cash flow of $50.3B, up 83.7% YoY, supported by higher net income and working capital management.; Free cash flow of $48.6B after $1.8B in capital expenditures, providing substantial financial flexibility.
- Balance sheet: Not disclosed—balance sheet data not provided in the excerpts; only income statement and cash flow figures are available.

## Risks
- Forward-looking statements are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially. (Evidence: We discuss many of these risks, uncertainties and other factors in this Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q and our Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended January 25, 2026 in greater detail under the heading 'Risk Factors' of such reports.)
- NVIDIA's business is subject to risks associated with its expansion into data center-scale AI infrastructure. (Evidence: NVIDIA is now a data center-scale AI infrastructure company reshaping all industries.)

## Management Commentary
- Themes: NVIDIA has evolved from PC graphics to a data center-scale AI infrastructure company.; Sustained demand for 3D graphics and the scale of the gaming market provided a foundation for expansion into AI, data science, and autonomous vehicles.; The company leverages its GPU architecture to create platforms for scientific computing, AI, and digital twin applications.
- Capital allocation: Capital expenditures increased to $1.8B from $1.2B, supporting infrastructure and product development.; Free cash flow of $48.6B provides capacity for shareholder returns and strategic investments, though specific allocation details are not disclosed in the excerpts.
> "NVIDIA pioneered accelerated computing to help solve the most challenging computational problems." – Management (MD&A)
> "NVIDIA is now a data center-scale AI infrastructure company reshaping all industries." – Management (MD&A)

## Outlook
- Guidance: Not disclosed—no specific financial guidance provided in the excerpts.
- Tone: neutral
- Drivers: Not disclosed—management did not provide explicit forward-looking drivers in the excerpts.
- Watch items: Forward-looking statements are subject to risks and uncertainties as detailed in the Risk Factors section.

## Executive Summary
NVIDIA reports Q1 FY2027 revenue of $81.6B, up 85.2% YoY, driven by data center-scale AI infrastructure demand. Management's disclosed tone was positive. Revenue surged to $81.6B from $44.1B in the prior-year quarter, reflecting continued momentum in AI and accelerated computing.; Net income more than tripled to $58.3B, with diluted EPS of $2.40 compared to $0.77 a year ago.; Gross margin expanded to 74.9% from 60.5%, underscoring operating leverage and product mix shift toward higher-value platforms.; Operating cash flow reached $50.3B, enabling robust free cash flow of $48.6B after $1.8B in capital expenditures.

## Financials
- Revenue: $81.6B vs. $44.1B (+85.2% YoY) – Revenue nearly doubled, driven by data center-scale AI infrastructure demand.
- Gross Profit: $61.2B vs. $26.7B (+129.3% YoY) – Gross profit more than doubled, with margin expansion of 14.4 percentage points.
- Operating Income: $53.5B vs. $21.6B (+147.4% YoY) – Operating income surged, reflecting strong revenue growth and operating leverage.
- Net Income: $58.3B vs. $18.8B (+210.6% YoY) – Net income more than tripled, benefiting from higher revenue and improved margins.
- Diluted EPS: $2.40 vs. $0.77 (+211.7% YoY) – EPS growth outpaced net income due to share repurchases.
- Gross Margin: 74.9% vs. 60.5% (+14.4pp) – Significant margin expansion driven by product mix and scale.
- Operating Margin: 65.6% vs. 49.1% (+16.5pp) – Operating margin improved sharply as revenue growth outpaced operating expenses.
- Net Margin: 71.5% vs. 42.6% (+28.9pp) – Net margin benefited from higher operating income and other income.
- Operating Cash Flow: $50.3B vs. $27.4B (+83.7% YoY) – Cash generation remained strong, funding investments and shareholder returns.
- Capital Expenditures: $1.8B vs. $1.2B (+43.2% YoY) – CapEx increased to support infrastructure and product development.
- Free Cash Flow: $48.6B vs. $26.2B (+85.5% YoY) – FCF nearly doubled, providing ample liquidity for strategic initiatives.

- Cash flow: Operating cash flow of $50.3B, up 83.7% YoY, supported by higher net income and working capital management.; Free cash flow of $48.6B after $1.8B in capital expenditures, providing substantial financial flexibility.
